pairs.question: What was the title of Andronikos Palaiologos Kantakouzenos within the Byzantine Empire?
pairs.answer: Andronikos Palaiologos Kantakouzenos held the title of Grand Domestic of the Byzantine Empire.

pairs.question: When and how did Andronikos Palaiologos Kantakouzenos die?
pairs.answer: Andronikos Palaiologos Kantakouzenos died on June 4, 1453. He was executed by Ottoman Sultan Mehmet II five days after the Fall of Constantinople, which occurred on May 29, 1453.

pairs.question: What advice did Andronikos Palaiologos Kantakouzenos give to Emperor Constantine XI Palaiologos regarding his third marriage?
pairs.answer: Andronikos advised Emperor Constantine XI Palaiologos to take Anna, the daughter of Emperor David of Trebizond, as his third wife instead of Mara, the daughter of Đurađ Branković, despot of Serbia.

pairs.question: What treaty did Andronikos Palaiologos Kantakouzenos sign on behalf of the Byzantine Empire in April 1448?
pairs.answer: Andronikos Palaiologos Kantakouzenos signed a treaty between Byzantium and Venice in April 1448. This was done in place of Demetrios Palaiologos Kantakouzenos, who was indisposed at the time.

pairs.question: What mission did Emperor John VIII Palaiologos assign to Andronikos Palaiologos Kantakouzenos in the fall of 1436?
pairs.answer: Emperor John VIII Palaiologos sent Andronikos Palaiologos Kantakouzenos to persuade the Serbians to send a delegation to the Council of Ferrara-Florence concerning the Union of Churches. Historical records suggest this mission was aimed at fostering church unity between the Byzantine and Western churches.

pairs.question: How was Andronikos Palaiologos Kantakouzenos related to Đurađ Branković, the despot of Serbia?
pairs.answer: Andronikos Palaiologos Kantakouzenos was the brother of Irene Kantakouzene, who was married to Đurađ Branković. This familial connection made him the brother-in-law of the Despot of Serbia.

pairs.question: Why might Andronikos Palaiologos Kantakouzenos have opposed Emperor Constantine XI's marriage to Mara Branković?
pairs.answer: Andronikos likely opposed the marriage due to his familial ties to Đurađ Branković and his role in advising against the union. The Serbian church's refusal to participate in the Council of Ferrara-Florence may have also influenced his stance, as the council aimed to unite the churches and could have strained relations further.
